title: Official Site of NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
#  Welcome to the Mayor’s Office 
## Zohran Kwame Mamdani 
#### 112th Mayor of New York City
Zohran Mamdani was sworn in as Mayor of the City of New York on January 1st, 2026. Prior to becoming Mayor, he represented the 36th New York State Assembly District and its neighborhoods of Astoria, Ditmars-Steinway, and Astoria Heights.

title: Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
**NEW YORK** — In his first 100 days in office, New York City Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani moved swiftly to deliver on his promise to protect New Yorkers, advancing policies centered on health, safety, and dignity — especially for those most often left behind. “Protecting New Yorkers demands action rooted in justice, equity and care,” said **New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani**. “In our first 100 days, we took concrete steps to increase public health and safety, protect vulnerable and historically disenfranchised communities, and reaffirm New York as a place of refuge and possibility.”
**Advancing True Public Safety**
Mayor Mamdani advanced a comprehensive approach to public safety — one that prioritizes prevention, transparency and fairness. Actions include: 
  * Driving crime to historically low levels in the first three months, Driving crime to historically low levels in the first three months, including record-low incidents of murder and shootings.

title: Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
* Appointing the City’s first Deputy Mayor for Community Safety and creating the first-ever Office of Community Safety 
  * Codifying the release of body-worn camera footage within 30 days of critical incidents, strengthening accountability 
  * Ending criminal enforcement for low-level traffic offenses involving e-bike riders and cyclists

title: Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
**Protecting Immigrant New Yorkers**
In the face of escalating federal threats, the Mamdani administration acted decisively to defend immigrant communities and uphold New York’s legacy as a sanctuary city. Actions include: 
  * Launching a Know Your Rights campaign in 10 languages and distributing 30,000 flyers through houses of worship 
  * Signing Executive Order 13 to reinforce sanctuary protections by: 
    * Prohibiting ICE from entering City properties — including schools, shelters, and hospitals — without a judicial warrant 
    * Strengthening data privacy protections 
    * Ordering agency audits and creating a crisis-response task force

title: Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
**Reforming the Corrections System**
The Mamdani Administration committed to closing Rikers Island while advancing immediate reforms to ensure humane conditions for those in custody and those who work in the system. Actions include:
  * Ordering full compliance with the City’s ban on solitary confinement and Board of Correction minimum standards 
  * Appointing Stanley Richards as Department of Correction commissioner, the first formerly incarcerated person to lead the department 
  * Opening the first Outposted Therapeutic Housing Unit at NYC Health + Hospitals/Bellevue, expanding access to specialized care for the most clinically vulnerable at Rikers Island.

title: Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
**Responding to Severe Winter Storms**
During one of the harshest winters in recent years, the administration mobilized a large-scale response to keep New Yorkers safe and the city operational. Actions include: 
  * Expanding NotifyNYC to nearly 1.5 million subscribers 
  * Implementing the first-ever 24-hour Code Blue, opening new warming centers, deploying new warming buses and transitioning approximately 2,000 placements of unsheltered New Yorkers into shelter between January 19 and March 4

title: Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days - NYC Mayor's Office
speaker: Mamdani
text:
**Keeping New Yorkers Healthy**
The Mamdani Administration prioritized access to care and services that allow all New Yorkers to live with dignity. Actions include: 
  * Closing the dilapidated 30th Street Shelter, while maintaining overall shelter capacity 
  * Opening the City’s first-ever pet inclusive family shelter at Magnolia Gardens and a new HELP Women’s Intake Shelter in East New York 
  * Investing $20 million in perinatal and early childhood mental health through the Strong Foundations Initiative 
  * Launching a $1 million public campaign to promote vaccinations 
  * Expanding overdose prevention services and opening new youth health clinics in Brooklyn and Queens

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Kwame Mamdani**[[c]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-14) (born October 18, 1991) is an American politician who has served as the 112th [mayor of New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_New_York_City "Mayor of New York City") since January 2026. A member of the [Democratic Party](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_Party_\(United_States\) "Democratic Party \(United States\)") and the [Democratic Socialists of America](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_Socialists_of_America "Democratic Socialists of America"), Mamdani served from 2021 to 2025 as a member of the [New York State Assembly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") for the [36th district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York%27s_36th_State_Assembly_district "New York's 36th State Assembly district"), representing [Astoria, Queens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astoria,_Queens "Astoria, Queens"). Mamdani was born in [Kampala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kampala "Kampala") to Indian parents, academic [Mahmood Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahmood_Mamdani "Mahmood Mamdani") and filmmaker [Mira Nair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mira_Nair "Mira Nair"). When he was seven years old, after having spent three years in [Cape Town](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cape_Town "Cape Town"), he and his family moved to [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City").

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text: Mamdani graduated from the [Bronx High School of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronx_High_School_of_Science "Bronx High School of Science") before receiving a bachelor's degree with a major in [Africana studies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africana_studies "Africana studies") from [Bowdoin College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bowdoin_College "Bowdoin College") in 2014. After working as a housing counselor and [rapper](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rapper "Rapper"), Mamdani entered [New York City politics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_politics "New York City politics") as a campaign manager for [Khader El-Yateem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khader_El-Yateem "Khader El-Yateem") and [Ross Barkan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ross_Barkan "Ross Barkan"). He was first elected to the [New York State Assembly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") in 2020, defeating five-term incumbent [Aravella Simotas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aravella_Simotas "Aravella Simotas") in the Democratic primary. Representing Astoria and [Long Island City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Long_Island_City "Long Island City"), he was reelected without opposition in 2022 and 2024. In October 2024, Mamdani announced his candidacy for mayor of New York City in the [2025 election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election").

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text: A [democratic socialist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_socialism "Democratic socialism"), Mamdani campaigned on a [progressive](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressivism_in_the_United_States "Progressivism in the United States"), affordability-focused platform, supporting [fare-free city buses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fare-free_city_buses "Fare-free city buses"), universal [child care](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Child_care "Child care"), [city-owned grocery stores](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/City-owned_grocery_stores "City-owned grocery stores"), a rent freeze on rent-stabilized units, additional affordable housing units, and a $30 [minimum wage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minimum_wage_in_the_United_States "Minimum wage in the United States") by 2030. He also expressed support for [LGBTQ rights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LGBTQ_rights_in_the_United_States "LGBTQ rights in the United States"), comprehensive [public safety reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_reform_in_the_United_States "Police reform in the United States"), and tax increases on corporations and those earning above [$1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-net-worth_individual "High-net-worth individual"). He won [the Democratic primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_Democratic_mayoral_primary "2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary") in June 2025, defeating former governor [Andrew Cuomo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") in an upset, and was elected mayor in [the November general election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election"). He is New York City's first [Muslim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muslims "Muslims") and first [Asian American](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asian_Americans "Asian Americans") mayor.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
George's Grammar School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._George%27s_Grammar_School_\(Cape_Town\) "St. George's Grammar School \(Cape Town\)") in [Mowbray](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mowbray,_Cape_Town "Mowbray, Cape Town") from 1996 to 1998,[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Davids-2024-18)[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-28) during the early [post-apartheid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-apartheid "Post-apartheid") years. He later said that the experience of living in Cape Town "taught me what inequality looks like up close[[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-mtembu2025-29)
The family moved to the United States and settled in New York City when Mamdani was seven,[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-30) and he was raised in Manhattan's [Morningside Heights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Morningside_Heights "Morningside Heights") neighborhood.[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Haag-2025-31) He has called his upbringing "privileged", saying, "I never had to want for something, and yet I knew that was not in any way the reality for most New Yorkers."[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-32) As a child, he was often present on his mother's film sets, where he was loved by members of the film crews, who variously called him "Z", "Zoru", "Fadoose", and "Nonstop Mamdani".[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-lahr2002-27)
Mamdani attended the [Bank Street School for Children](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank_Street_College_of_Education#Bank_Street_School_for_Children "Bank Street College of Education") on the [Upper West Side](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Upper_West_Side "Upper West Side") of Manhattan, where he successfully ran as the independent candidate in a middle school [mock election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mock_election "Mock election"), adopting a platform of "equal rights, anti-war policies that proposed spending money on education rather than the military".[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-33) In 2003, he returned to Kampala for a year and attended school during his father's [sabbatical](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sabbatical "Sabbatical") there;[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Chen-2016-20) his paternal grandparents and aunt still lived there and helped take care of him while his father was working on the book _Good Muslim, Bad Muslim_.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-34)
In 2010, Mamdani graduated from the [Bronx High School of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronx_High_School_of_Science "Bronx High School of Science") in [Kingsbridge Heights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingsbridge_Heights "Kingsbridge Heights"), [Bronx](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronx "Bronx"),[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-35)[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-36)[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Saltonstall-2025-37) where he co-founded the school's first [cricket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") team[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Marcelo-2025-16) and unsuccessfully ran for student body vice president.[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-38) He also played soccer with the West Side Soccer League.[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Saltonstall-2025-37)
Mamdani attended [Bowdoin College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bowdoin_College "Bowdoin College") in [Brunswick, Maine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brunswick,_Maine "Brunswick, Maine"), where he co-founded the school's chapter of [Students for Justice in Palestine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Students_for_Justice_in_Palestine "Students for Justice in Palestine").[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Shoki-2020-39)[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sorkin-2019-40) He was a regular contributor to the campus newspaper _[The Bowdoin Orient](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bowdoin_Orient "The Bowdoin Orient")_ , covering politics, culture, and sports in his columns, "Kwame's Kolumnalu" and "Talk of the Quad".[[38]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-41) In January 2014, he co-authored an [op-ed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Op-ed "Op-ed") in the _[Bangor Daily News](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangor_Daily_News "Bangor Daily News")_ urging Bowdoin to join the [American Studies Association's boycott of Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_Studies_Association%27s_boycott_of_Israel "American Studies Association's boycott of Israel") and criticizing the college's president, [Barry Mills](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Mills_\(college_president\) "Barry Mills \(college president\)").[[39]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-42) Mamdani graduated from Bowdoin in 2014 with a bachelor's degree in [Africana studies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africana_studies "Africana studies").[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Shoki-2020-39)[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sorkin-2019-40)
## Early career
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Zohran_Mamdani_on_TMTv-SA.jpg/250px-Zohran_Mamdani_on_TMTv-SA.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Zohran_Mamdani_on_TMTv-SA.jpg)Mamdani at the 2016 Johannesburg premiere of _Queen of Katwe_
Mamdani is a fan of [hip-hop](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") and has composed, performed, and produced rap music.[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Green-2025-43) Under the moniker Young Cardamom, he collaborated with his best friend, Ugandan rapper HAB (Abdul Bar Hussein), whose origins are in [South Sudan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Sudan "South Sudan"),[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-brara-44)[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rap2015-45) as Young Cardamom & HAB.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
Their first song, "Kanda [Chap Chap]", was about [chapati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chapati "Chapati"), an [Indian flatbread](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_flatbread "Indian flatbread").[[43]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Onmanorama2025-46) They performed tracks from their 2016 [EP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EP "EP") _Sidda Mukyaalo_ ("No going back to the village") at the [Nyege Nyege](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nyege_Nyege "Nyege Nyege") festival.[[44]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-47)[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Waswa-2025-48) The pair rapped in languages including [Nubi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nubi "Nubi"), [Luganda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luganda "Luganda"), [Swahili](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Swahili "Swahili"), and English, partly to create a unique Ugandan style of rap rather than imitating American rap, and partly to convey that Ugandan residents with roots in other countries are all Ugandan. The chapati was chosen as a symbol because it originates in the [Indian subcontinent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_subcontinent "Indian subcontinent") but has become a Ugandan staple.[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rap2015-45) In their music, they addressed social issues in Uganda, such as corruption and "black and brown relations", as well as [colonialism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colonisation_of_Africa "Colonisation of Africa").[[46]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-int2016-49) Young Cardamom & HAB were nominated for "Rookie of the Year" at the inaugural Ugandan (UG) Hip Hop Awards.[[47]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-50)[[48]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-51)
Mamdani curated and produced [the soundtrack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_of_Katwe_\(Original_Motion_Picture_Soundtrack\) "Queen of Katwe \(Original Motion Picture Soundtrack\)") for his mother Mira Nair's 2016 film _[Queen of Katwe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_of_Katwe "Queen of Katwe")_ , for which he was nominated for a 2017 [Guild of Music Supervisors Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guild_of_Music_Supervisors_Awards "Guild of Music Supervisors Awards");[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-52)[[50]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-53)[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-disney-54) he co-wrote the song "#1 Spice" with HAB for the film.[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-brara-44)[[52]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-55) Mamdani also appears as an extra in the film,[[53]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-tusing2025-56) and is credited as [third assistant director](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third_assistant_director "Third assistant director").[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-disney-54) Nair subsequently offered him parts in the stage musical adaptation of her film _[Monsoon Wedding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monsoon_Wedding "Monsoon Wedding")_ , after he took part in [stage readings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stage_reading "Stage reading") for the show, and her [television adaptation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_Suitable_Boy_\(TV_series\) "A Suitable Boy \(TV series\)") of the [Vikram Seth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vikram_Seth "Vikram Seth") novel _[A Suitable Boy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_Suitable_Boy "A Suitable Boy")_ , but he declined.[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-57)[[55]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-58)
In 2017, Mamdani released the song "Salaam" under the name Zohran Kwame.[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-59) In the song, Mamdani praised the Holy Land Five, five people arrested and connected to the [Holy Land Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_Land_Foundation_for_Relief_and_Development "Holy Land Foundation for Relief and Development").[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-60)[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-61) In April 2019, under the moniker Mr.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
Cardamom, he released the single "Nani", an homage to his grandmother. Actress and food writer [Madhur Jaffrey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhur_Jaffrey "Madhur Jaffrey") portrays Mamdani's grandmother in the video,[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rayner2019-62)[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-63)[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-64)[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-65) which pays tribute to Jaffrey and [New York's South Asian culture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asians_in_New_York_City "Asians in New York City").[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rayner2019-62)
Mamdani entered [New York City politics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_politics "New York City politics") as a volunteer for Ali Najmi's campaign in the [2015 special election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City%27s_23rd_City_Council_district#2015_special "New York City's 23rd City Council district") for the [23rd district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City%27s_23rd_City_Council_district "New York City's 23rd City Council district") of the City Council.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17) Mamdani was inspired to join Najmi's campaign after learning that he was supported by [Heems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heems "Heems"), a New York rapper of Indian descent and co-founder of alternative hip-hop group [Das Racist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Das_Racist "Das Racist").[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Green-2025-43)[[63]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-66) Specifically, Mamdani attributes his involvement in local politics to a 2015 _[The Village Voice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Village_Voice "The Village Voice")_ article about Najmi and Heems, whom he described as one of his favorite rappers.[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-67)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-68)
In 2017, Mamdani joined the New York City chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) and worked for the campaign of [New York City Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Council "New York City Council") candidate [Khader El-Yateem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khader_El-Yateem "Khader El-Yateem"), a [Palestinian Lutheran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palestinian_Christians "Palestinian Christians") [minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minister_\(Christianity\) "Minister \(Christianity\)") and democratic socialist from [Bay Ridge, Brooklyn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bay_Ridge,_Brooklyn "Bay Ridge, Brooklyn").[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17) Part of Mamdani's motivation for joining the DSA was its pro-Palestine stance, which aligned with his own prior activism.[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-69) Mamdani served as the campaign manager for [Ross Barkan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ross_Barkan "Ross Barkan")'s 2018 unsuccessful bid for the [New York State Senate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Senate "New York State Senate") and was also a field organizer for fellow democratic socialist [Tiffany Cabán](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tiffany_Cab%C3%A1n "Tiffany Cabán")'s close-run but also unsuccessful 2019 campaign for [Queens County district attorney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queens_County_district_attorney "Queens County district attorney").[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17)[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Mamdani-2021-70)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-71)
Starting in 2018,[[69]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-72) Mamdani worked as a [foreclosure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foreclosure "Foreclosure") prevention and housing counselor.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
There, he assisted lower-income immigrant homeowners in [Queens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queens "Queens") with [eviction notices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eviction_notice "Eviction notice") and efforts to prevent them from being evicted from their homes. He said the experience motivated him to run for office to address the housing and affordability crisis.[[70]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-73)
Further information: [Electoral history of Zohran Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electoral_history_of_Zohran_Mamdani "Electoral history of Zohran Mamdani")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Zohran_Mamdani_at_the_Resist_Fascism_Rally_in_Bryant_Park_on_Oct_27th_2024.jpg/250px-Zohran_Mamdani_at_the_Resist_Fascism_Rally_in_Bryant_Park_on_Oct_27th_2024.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Zohran_Mamdani_at_the_Resist_Fascism_Rally_in_Bryant_Park_on_Oct_27th_2024.jpg)Mamdani at the Resist Fascism Rally in [Bryant Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bryant_Park "Bryant Park"), October 27, 2024
In October 2019, Mamdani announced his campaign to represent New York's 36th State Assembly district, which encompasses Astoria and [Long Island City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Long_Island_City "Long Island City") in Queens.[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kaufman-2020-74)[[72]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-75) He was endorsed by the DSA,[[73]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-76) running on a platform of [housing reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Right_to_housing "Right to housing"), [police](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_reform_in_the_United_States "Police reform in the United States") and [prison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prisoner_rights_in_the_United_States "Prisoner rights in the United States") reform, and [public ownership of utilities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_utility "Public utility").[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kaufman-2020-74) Mamdani's June 2020 [primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Primary_election "Primary election") victory over five-term Democratic incumbent [Aravella Simotas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aravella_Simotas "Aravella Simotas") took almost a month to call,[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-77) and he won the general election with no [Republican](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Republican_Party_\(United_States\) "Republican Party \(United States\)") opposition in November.[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-78) Mamdani was reelected without opposition in 2022 and 2024.[[76]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-79)[[77]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-80)
Mamdani was a member of the DSA's nine-member[[d]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-81) ["State Socialists in Office" bloc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Socialists_in_Office "New York State Socialists in Office") in New York and a member of the Muslim Democratic Club of New York.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17)[[78]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-82)[[79]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sterne-2024-83) He was the keynote speaker at the 2023 DSA convention, saying, "We are special as DSA electeds not because of ourselves; we are special because of our organization".[[80]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-84)[[81]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-85)[[82]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-86)
By January 2025, Mamdani was a member of nine Assembly committees: the Committee on Aging; the Committee on Cities; the Committee on Election Law; the Committee on Energy; the Committee on Real Property Taxation; the Black, Puerto Rican, Hispanic & Asian Legislative Caucus; the Puerto Rican/Hispanic Task Force; the Asian Pacific American Task Force; and the Task Force on New Americans.[[83]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-87)
By May 2025, Mamdani had been the primary sponsor of 20 bills in the Assembly—three of which became law—and the co-sponsor of 238 bills.[[84]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-88) As a member of the Assembly, he helped launch a successful fare-free bus pilot program and participated in a [hunger strike](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hunger_strike "Hunger strike") alongside taxi drivers.[[85]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-89)[[86]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-90) Due to his campaign, Mamdani had the most absences from the Assembly session of any Democrat, and the second-most overall, after [David McDonough](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_McDonough "David McDonough").[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-91)
## Mayor of New York City
### 2025 campaign
Main articles: [2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_Democratic_mayoral_primary "2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary") and [2025 New York City mayoral election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election")
On October 23, 2024, Mamdani announced his candidacy for [mayor of New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_New_York_City "Mayor of New York City").[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-92) His platform included support for free city buses and a rent freeze in rent-stabilized housing.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Mays-2025-93)[[90]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-94) Mamdani also wants the city government to operate five [grocery stores](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grocery_store "Grocery store")—one in each borough—to drive down grocery prices.[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-95) His platform included support for universal child care and the construction of 200,000 new affordable housing units.[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-96) He also supports [public safety reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_reform_in_the_United_States "Police reform in the United States") and a $30 [minimum wage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minimum_wage_in_the_United_States "Minimum wage in the United States") by 2030.[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-97) His platform called for tax increases on corporations and on those earning above $1[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-98)
For most of the primary campaign, Mamdani trailed former New York governor [Andrew Cuomo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") in polling.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
But many Jewish leaders and voters remained critical of him.[[108]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Yachot-2025-112) Mamdani's campaign launch drew media attention for its use of [Bollywood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bollywood "Bollywood") songs and cultural references, which aimed to engage younger and immigrant voters.[[109]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-113)[[110]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-114)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/28/20250926New_York%2C_Estados_Unidos-_Reuni%C3%B3n_bilateral_con_el_candidato_a_la_Alcald%C3%ADa_de_Nueva_York%2C_Zohran_Mamdani_-_Juan_Cano09326.jpg/250px-20250926New_York%2C_Estados_Unidos-_Reuni%C3%B3n_bilateral_con_el_candidato_a_la_Alcald%C3%ADa_de_Nueva_York%2C_Zohran_Mamdani_-_Juan_Cano09326.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:20250926New_York,_Estados_Unidos-_Reuni%C3%B3n_bilateral_con_el_candidato_a_la_Alcald%C3%ADa_de_Nueva_York,_Zohran_Mamdani_-_Juan_Cano09326.jpg)Mamdani with Colombian President [Gustavo Petro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gustavo_Petro "Gustavo Petro") in New York City, September 26, 2025
During the primary, Mamdani's campaign was supported by the _New Yorkers for Lower Costs_ super PAC, which spent approximately $1.3 million supporting him and opposing [Cuomo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") before the primary and raised an additional $1 million afterward. The super PAC received $100,000 in contributions from the Unity and Justice Fund in May and June 2025.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
New York Attorney General [Letitia James](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letitia_James "Letitia James") administered the oath of office in the abandoned [City Hall subway station](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/City_Hall_station_\(IRT_Lexington_Avenue_Line\) "City Hall station \(IRT Lexington Avenue Line\)") just after midnight.[[139]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-143) Mamdani took the oath on the [Quran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quran "Quran"), making him the first mayor of the city to do so. He used two copies of the book, his grandfather's and [Arturo Alfonso Schomburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arturo_Alfonso_Schomburg "Arturo Alfonso Schomburg")'s.[[140]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-144) A public inauguration was held in the afternoon, at which Mamdani was sworn in by [Bernie Sanders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bernie_Sanders "Bernie Sanders").[[141]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-145)[[142]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-146) Speakers and performers at the inauguration included Sanders, [Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexandria_Ocasio-Cortez "Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez"), [Javier Muñoz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Javier_Mu%C3%B1oz_\(actor\) "Javier Muñoz \(actor\)"), [Lucy Dacus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucy_Dacus "Lucy Dacus"), and [Mandy Patinkin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandy_Patinkin "Mandy Patinkin").[[143]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-147)
Mamdani's first act as mayor, immediately after taking the oath of office, was to appoint transportation consultant, educator, and former [NYC Department of Transportation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Department_of_Transportation "New York City Department of Transportation") director of capital planning and project management [Mike Flynn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mike_Flynn_\(transportation_official\) "Mike Flynn \(transportation official\)") as his Department of Transportation commissioner.[[144]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-148) His first [executive order](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Executive_order "Executive order") was to revoke all executive orders his predecessor [Eric Adams](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eric_Adams "Eric Adams") made after he was [indicted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Investigations_into_the_Eric_Adams_administration#Indictment "Investigations into the Eric Adams administration") on bribery charges on September 25, 2024.[[145]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-149)[[146]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-150)[[147]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-151)[[148]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-152)
One week after his inauguration, Mamdani and Governor [Kathy Hochul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kathy_Hochul "Kathy Hochul") announced a plan to expand free and low-cost childcare in New York City, with the aim to eventually provide universal free childcare.[[149]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-153) One of Mamdani's first major challenges as mayor was managing the effects of a [January 2026 winter storm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/January_23%E2%80%9327,_2026_North_American_winter_storm "January 23–27, 2026 North American winter storm"), which killed 14 people in New York City.[[150]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-154)[[151]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-155)[[152]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-156)
#### Gracie Mansion bombing attempt
Main article: [2026 New York City bombing attempt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_New_York_City_bombing_attempt "2026 New York City bombing attempt")
On March 7, 2026, a [homemade explosive device](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Improvised_explosive_device "Improvised explosive device") was thrown into the crowd of an anti-Islam protest organized by far-right activist and [January 6 United States Capitol attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/January_6_United_States_Capitol_attack "January 6 United States Capitol attack") rioter [Jake Lang](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jake_Lang "Jake Lang") outside the mayor's official residence, [Gracie Mansion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gracie_Mansion "Gracie Mansion").[[153]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-157) Police investigated the attack as an "act of ISIS-inspired terrorism".

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
Two men, Emir Balat and Ibrahim Kayumi, were taken into custody,[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-:0-158) and the [U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._Attorney_for_the_Southern_District_of_New_York "U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York") brought federal charges against them.[[155]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-159) Mamdani condemned the protest, calling it "appalling" and a display of anti-Muslim bigotry rooted in racism,[[156]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-RichardLuscombe-160) and also called Lang a "[white supremacist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_supremacist "White supremacist")".[[157]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-MamadaniTwitter-161)[[158]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-162) He added that the "right to peaceful protest is sacred" and "does not belong only to those we agree with".[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-:0-158)

Main article: [Political positions of Zohran Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Political_positions_of_Zohran_Mamdani "Political positions of Zohran Mamdani")

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia

text:
As a mayoral candidate, he instead emphasized building a cooperative relationship with the department on violent crime prevention while reducing the involvement of police in other matters.[[180]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-185)[[181]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-186)[[182]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-187)
Mamdani has condemned dictatorships in [Venezuela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") and [Cuba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuba "Cuba") while criticizing US sanctions on those countries.[[183]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-188) After [the U.S. captured Nicolas Maduro and his wife](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_strikes_in_Venezuela "2026 United States strikes in Venezuela"), Mamdani called the action "a violation of federal and international law".[[184]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-189)[[185]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-190) In January 2026, Mamdani criticized the [Iranian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran "Iran") regime's [violence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_massacres "2026 Iran massacres") against [anti-government protesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests").[[186]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-191) He has denounced Indian prime minister [Narendra Modi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Narendra_Modi "Narendra Modi") as a "war criminal"[[187]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-192)[[188]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-193)[[189]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-194) and remains a critic of Israel and the [Gaza genocide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_genocide "Gaza genocide"),[[190]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-195)[[191]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-196)[[192]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-197) describing its policies as [apartheid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_apartheid "Israeli apartheid")[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Glueck-202522-198) and supporting the movement to [boycott, divest from, and sanction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boycott,_Divestment_and_Sanctions "Boycott, Divestment and Sanctions") the country.[[194]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-199) He co-sponsored the "Not on our dime!" bill to stop New York charities from funding [Israeli settler violence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_settler_violence "Israeli settler violence").[[195]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-:02-200) After the [October 7 attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/October_7_attacks "October 7 attacks"), Mamdani mourned all victims, condemned [Hamas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamas "Hamas")'s attacks as war crimes,[[196]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-201)[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Glueck-202522-198)[[197]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kornbluh-2025b22-202) and called for both sides to "lay down their arms" while advocating a permanent [ceasefire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calls_for_a_ceasefire_during_the_Gaza_war "Calls for a ceasefire during the Gaza war"),[[198]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-203) the end of [Israeli occupation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_occupation "Israeli occupation"), and the end of [ethnocratic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethnocratic "Ethnocratic") policy in favor of "equal rights for all".[[199]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-204)[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Glueck-202522-198)[[200]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-205) He said he would attempt to honor the [International Criminal Court](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Criminal_Court "International Criminal Court")'s warrants for [Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Criminal_Court_arrest_warrants_for_Israeli_leaders "International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Israeli leaders") and [Russian president Vladimir Putin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Criminal_Court_arrest_warrants_for_Russian_leaders "International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Russian leaders") if they visit New York City.[[201]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-206)
Mamdani connects environmental justice to social equity.[[202]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-207) He opposed expansion of the gas-fired [Astoria Energy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Poletti_Power_Project "Charles Poletti Power Project") power plant,[[203]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-208)[[204]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-209) supports the All-Electric Buildings Act and [congestion pricing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congestion_pricing_in_New_York_City "Congestion pricing in New York City"), and proposed a "Green Schools for a Healthier New York City" plan to retrofit schools with [solar panels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_panels "Solar panels"), create green schoolyards, and establish resilience hubs.[[205]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sim%C3%B5es-20252-210)[[206]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-211)[[207]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-212) His platform includes universal [pre-K](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pre-K "Pre-K"), [baby baskets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maternity_package "Maternity package") for new families, defending Hasidic [yeshivas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yeshivas "Yeshivas")' autonomy,[[208]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kornbluh-2025a-213) expanding sanctuary protections for immigrants, making New York City an [LGBTQ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LGBTQ "LGBTQ")+ sanctuary city with an Office of LGBTQIA+ Affairs, and eliminating bus fares through expanded [MTA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metropolitan_Transit_Authority_\(New_York\) "Metropolitan Transit Authority \(New York\)") funding and free transit initiatives.[[f]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-219)
After the deaths of [Renée Good](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ren%C3%A9e_Good "Renée Good") and [Alex Pretti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_of_Alex_Pretti "Killing of Alex Pretti"), who were both killed by federal border patrol agents in January 2026, Mamdani took a strong stance against [ICE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ICE "ICE").

title: Zohran Mamdani - Wikipedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
supporters") of the [English football](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Premier_League "Premier League") team [Arsenal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arsenal_F.C. "Arsenal F.C.")[[227]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-233)[[228]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-234) and the [New York Knicks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_Knicks "New York Knicks") basketball team, whose games he has attended as part of past campaigns,[[229]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-235) and also follows [cricket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cricket "Cricket")[[230]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-236) and [professional wrestling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Professional_wrestling "Professional wrestling").[[231]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-237) He is a shareholder of the Spanish soccer team [Real Oviedo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real_Oviedo "Real Oviedo"), having been one of 20,000 people to purchase shares in the club in 2012 as part of a fundraising drive to stave off bankruptcy.[[232]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-238)[[233]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-239)
## In popular culture
Mamdani was [Google](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google "Google")'s top "People" trending search for 2025. His [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia "Wikipedia") article was among the 10 most-read in 2025.[[234]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-ZohranMamdaniMostSearchedMayor-240)
In the [51st season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live_season_51 "Saturday Night Live season 51") of _[Saturday Night Live](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")_ , [Ramy Youssef](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramy_Youssef "Ramy Youssef") portrayed Mamdani in a sketch based on the [second debate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election#Debates "2025 New York City mayoral election") of the [2025 New York City mayoral election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election") _._[[235]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-241)

## Notes
  1. **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_ref-1)** Elected on both Democratic and WFP ballot lines in New York via [electoral fusion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electoral_fusion#New_York "Electoral fusion").
  2. **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_ref-2)** The Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) are not a registered political party. Instead, they are a political organization for those with democratic socialist ideologies. Mamdani is a member of and is endorsed by the [New York City DSA chapter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Democratic_Socialists_of_America "New York City Democratic Socialists of America").

**Zohran Mamdani** ([Democratic Party](https://ballotpedia.org/Democratic_Party)) is the [Mayor of New York](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York,_New_York). He assumed office on January 1, 2026. His current term ends on January 1, 2030. Mamdani (Democratic Party, Working Families Party) ran for election for [Mayor of New York](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York,_New_York). He won in the general election on [November 4, 2025](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York_\(2025\)). He advanced from the Democratic primary on [June 24, 2025](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York_\(2025\)).

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
Mamdani announced that he was running for New York City mayor in October 2024.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1) In the [Democratic primary](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York,_2025_\(June_24_Democratic_primary\) "Mayoral election in New York, New York, 2025 \(June 24 Democratic primary\)"), he defeated former governor [Andrew Cuomo](https://ballotpedia.org/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") and nine other candidates, receiving 56.4% of the vote after three rounds of [ranked-choice voting](https://ballotpedia.org/Ranked-choice_voting "Ranked-choice voting"). In the [2025 general election](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York_\(2025\) "Mayoral election in New York, New York \(2025\)"), he received 50.9% of the vote, defeating Cuomo, who ran as an independent, and [Curtis Sliwa](https://ballotpedia.org/Curtis_Sliwa "Curtis Sliwa") (R). Mamdani is the first Muslim and the first South Asian person to serve as mayor of New York City. [[2]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-2)
Mamdani was born in Kampala, Uganda, and moved to New York City with his family when he was seven years old.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
He attended Bronx High School of Science and went on to earn a bachelor’s degree in Africana Studies from Bowdoin College in 2014.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1)
In college, Mamdani cofounded his school's first Students for Justice in Palestine chapter and worked for _The Bowdoin Orient,_ the college's student newspaper and literary magazine.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1)[[3]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-assemblybio-3) After graduating, Mamdani worked as a foreclosure prevention housing counselor in Queens.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1)
Mamdani was first elected to the [New York Assembly](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") in [2020](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York_State_Assembly_elections,_2020 "New York State Assembly elections, 2020"). He defeated ten-year incumbent [Aravella Simotas](https://ballotpedia.org/Aravella_Simotas "Aravella Simotas") 51.2% to 48.6% in the Democratic primary and was uncontested in the general election.[[3]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-assemblybio-3) According to _Time Magazine,_ Mamdani's main focuses as a state legislator were housing and transportation reform.[[4]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-4)
Mamdani completed [Ballotpedia's Candidate Connection](https://ballotpedia.org/Ballotpedia%27s_Candidate_Connection "Ballotpedia's Candidate Connection") survey in 2020.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
As part of his response to the question "What areas of public policy are you personally passionate about?," Mamdani wrote, "I know that we'll never win the rights we all deserve - rights to healthcare, housing, education, nutrition, childcare, and public power - without a grassroots movement of the working class rising up to demand them." 
Mamdani describes himself as a Democratic socialist. In a 2020 campaign video, he said, "I’m a Democratic socialist, and what socialism means to me is a commitment to dignity, a state that provides whatever is necessary for its people to live a dignified life."[[5]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-5)
During his mayoral campaign, Mamdani said he wanted to "freeze the rent for all stabilized tenants," "permanently eliminate the fare on every city bus," "implement free childcare for every New Yorker aged 6 weeks to 5 years," and "create a network of city-owned grocery stores."[[6]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-6)
## Contents

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
## Biography
Zohran Kwame Mamdani was born in Kampala, Uganda.[[7]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bio-7)[[8]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bio2-8) Mamdani graduated from Bronx High School of Science. He earned a bachelor's degree in Africana studies from Bowdoin College. Mamdani's career experience includes working as a foreclosure prevention housing counselor.[[8]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bio2-8)

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
Zohran Mamdani completed [Ballotpedia's Candidate Connection](https://ballotpedia.org/Ballotpedia%27s_Candidate_Connection) survey in 2020. The survey questions appear in bold and are followed by Mamdani's responses. Expand all | Collapse all
I was born and raised in Kampala, Uganda, and grew up in New York City after my family moved here when I was seven years old. I graduated from Bronx Science and now work as a housing counselor, helping immigrant families facing eviction stay in the homes they worked their whole lives to earn. I am running for State Assembly because it's time to guarantee housing to all New Yorkers as a right, regardless of ability to pay. It's time to desegregate our schools, fully eliminate cash bail, ban solitary confinement, fund and fix the MTA, end workplace discrimination, and fight for social, racial, economic, and environmental justice for the many, not the few.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text: * We need to stop treating housing as a commodity from which private developers can extract exorbitant profits, and start guaranteeing it as a human right for all. * Energy should be a public resource, not a for-profit commodity. We need to eliminate the profit motive from our energy system and empower the working class to reverse decades of disinvestment in frontline communities that have borne the brunt of environmental degradation and who are most imminently threatened by accelerating climate catastrophe. * I believe that people should not have to endure the violence and coercion of a criminal-legal system that props up the exploitation of the market by surveilling, caging, and killing those fighting to survive under capitalism.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text: The housing crisis we're facing in NY isn't restricted to low-income homeowners; it's impacting renters too. Half of all tenants in Astoria are spending a third or more of their income on rent. I spend more than half of my paycheck on rent, just like a quarter of my neighbors. Yet our landlords can refuse to renew our leases, evict us without good cause, and raise our rents whenever they like to prices we can't afford to pay.

title: Zohran Mamdani - Ballotpedia
speaker: Mamdani
text:
I know that we'll never win the rights we all deserve - rights to healthcare, housing, education, nutrition, childcare, and public power - without a grassroots movement of the working class rising up to demand them. Another world is possible, but only if we fight for it together. _**Note:** Ballotpedia reserves the right to edit Candidate Connection survey responses. Any edits made by Ballotpedia will be clearly marked with [brackets] for the public. If the candidate disagrees with an edit, he or she may request the full removal of the survey response from Ballotpedia.org. Ballotpedia does not edit or correct typographical errors unless the candidate's campaign requests it._

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani**, a State Assembly Member representing the 36th District in Queens and a candidate in the June 2025 Democratic primary for Mayor, spoke with The New York Editorial Board on the morning of January 30, 2025. _(photo by Juan Manuel Benítez)_ _Participating journalists: Juan Manuel Benítez, Nicole Gelinas, Josh Greenman, Christina Greer, Alyssa Katz, Ben Max, Akash Mehta, Harry Siegel, Ben Smith, Liena Zagare._ **Full Transcript** _**Qualifications for Mayor / Management**_ **Ben Max** Good morning. Thanks for being here. So would you tell us just very specifically what you think are two, maybe three, of the most important qualifications for being Mayor of New York City and how you meet those?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** I think at its core, a mayor is a messenger, a delegate, a liaison. In my terms in the Assembly, I believe that I have lived up to those requirements, most especially through launching campaigns, often from scratch. Most notably, the “Fix the MTA” campaign, I don't think I need to explain why we called it that, where we won more than $100 million in increased subway service, bus service, a reduction in the fare hike, as well as New York City's [first-ever free bus pilot](https://www.mta.info/guides/riding-the-bus/fare-free-bus-pilot).

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: And in doing those things, we delivered tangible benefits to working-class New Yorkers. Namely, putting tens of millions of dollars back in their pockets and seeing immense increases in ridership, up to 30% across those five bus routes, one in each borough as well as a significant decrease in assaults on bus operators, 38.9% across those routes. And an illustration of the fact that in a time of the climate crisis, we have a lot of options in front of us that can actually reduce a lot of the carbon emissions across our city, and one of them is when you make buses free. We saw that 11% of new riders got out of their cars or taxis they were taking and got onto public transit.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: So I do believe that through my work in the Assembly, especially showcased with “Fix the MTA,” I’ve shown myself adept at the skills required. And I think of \[Mayor\] Michelle Wu in Boston in many ways as a model for the kind of work you do as a legislator, then becoming a mayor, and also speaking to the possibility of being a mayor at around the same age as her. **Josh Greenman** You used three words, but you didn't use the word manager. It's a huge government. **Zohran Mamdani** Yes, I think there is absolutely a necessity of competency and of being a competent manager, specifically. I also think that you need to understand where your knowledge goes up until and who you need to help you to go beyond that.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: I have seen in previous mayoral administrations, across the ideological spectrum, examples that I would love to bring into my administration of how you can empower the people who know the most about the work that you're seeking to accomplish. I think that it is especially critical in a time when we have a mayor who views himself as more of a monarch, as someone who must answer every question, who must decide every agency directive, who must be in the room deciding whether or not an elected official can even meet with that agency, that we have someone who will empower the New Yorkers who are the best and brightest of our city. And that we give them the authority to actually follow through on that mandate.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: And furthermore, I do not believe that every single head of an agency needs to align with me 100% across every single issue, but rather specifically on that issue of that agency. For example, I'm a big critic, often, of Michael Bloomberg’s mayoralty. I also look with deep admiration at his empowering of Janette Sadik-Khan to be the head of the Department of Transportation, and how that reshaped the streetscape across New York City. And I think that those are the kinds of examples. Those are also the kinds of individuals that I think we have to bring into the next administration, as opposed to people that you want to give patronage to at every single turn.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Ben Smith** Have you talked to Janette Sadik-Khan? **Zohran Mamdani** I haven't, but I hope that by mentioning her here, she will reach out. _**Obstacles to Progressive Leadership / Working with Albany**_ **Akash Mehta** I want to ask about one of those past administrations. Bill de Blasio came into office as an unlikely progressive insurgent telling a “tale of two cities.” He had major accomplishments, but the central problem that he set out to solve, income inequality, by some metrics didn't budge over the course of his tenure. What do you think prevented his administration from more fully transforming the city, and how would you overcome the same obstacles?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** To spell out some of the successes that you are alluding to, I think that standing up universal pre-K and the beginnings of 3-K is an incredible accomplishment. Because alongside my very clear and explicit platform of freezing the rent, making buses fast and free and providing universal child care — whether your child is six weeks or five years — is a belief that government can and must do big things. That it must show itself able to intervene in the lives of working-class people and provide them with relief at the very least.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: And I think that those accomplishments of the de Blasio administration with universal pre-K, also with multiple rent freezes, did provide that to millions of New Yorkers. And we're talking about an expense when it comes to child care that can be $20,000-$25,000. In the broader analysis of income inequality across New York City, I think that some of this also has to do with being mayor at the time of a governor \[Andrew Cuomo\] who was hell-bent on a very different kind of politics. A governor who chases press releases and publicity more than the actual results of those programs, and one who for many years refused to even acknowledge that the state ran the MTA. And I think that some of those structural factors do create limitations.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: And I also think that there were some approaches in the de Blasio administration that I did disagree with. I say that as someone, sitting in front of incredible food, who was on a 15-day hunger strike protesting the de Blasio administration's approach to debt forgiveness for thousands of working-class taxi drivers. And I’m proud that after that two-and-a-half-week strike which had already followed civil disobedience of myself and five other elected officials being arrested outside of City Hall, which had already followed 45 days of consecutive protests, which had already followed years of the New York Taxi Workers Alliance fighting for this issue, the de Blasio administration did change its course and we secured more than $450 million in debt relief.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: So I think that obviously there are things that could have been done differently. I also think that there are a lot of things to acknowledge and appreciate, especially in light of the administration we've had since. **Josh Greenman** What’s the answer to the question of how to do better than he did? Is there an answer to the question about how to do better in closing the income gap specifically, to overcome the obstacles that he did encounter?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** I think part of it is expanding the approach and I think that’s why you see that our campaign is built around multiple prongs — ways that we would intervene. I think acknowledging the successes there and trying to go beyond that. And also I think that given how much New York City is a creature of the state, I think my record in Albany and my ability to build coalitions across the ideological spectrum is one that would serve our city well in securing a lot of the support necessary to enact multiple parts of this agenda.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Ben Smith** Do you think you’d be able to do that with [‘Governor \[Mike\] Lawler’](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-11-20/mike-lawler-weighs-challenging-kathy-hochul-for-ny-governor)? **Zohran Mamdani** I think that there’s possibility in every politician. I think there are some that are much more difficult, especially when they are those outside of the Democratic Party. **Ben Smith** How would you approach him? **Zohran Mamdani** **‘** Governor Lawler’? **Ben Smith** Yes. **Zohran Mamdani** You know, I think ‘Governor Lawler’ — let me say Congressmember Lawler. We, the two of us, have been confused often because we came into Albany at the same time and both had beards. And there was one point, actually, where another member spoke to me for five minutes thinking that I was Mike Lawler.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: Look, Lawler is a member of a party whose presidential nominee was elected, and part of the reason he was elected was on a message of cheaper groceries. Now, I think there are absolutely horrific and heinous things about Donald Trump and Donald Trump’s platform that we need to fight at every point. And I also think what we need to do is, in the ways of Bernie Sanders, challenge his party to deliver on some of the things that they are lying about directly to working-class voters.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: I am a candidate sitting in front of you who has the only plan in this race to reduce the price of groceries. And I think one example of this is [putting it forward](https://www.nytimes.com/2024/12/12/nyregion/grocery-stores-city-owned.html) and saying, if you care so deeply about cheaper groceries, if you care so deeply about providing relief to working-class people, why will you not support the only plan here that we can actually guarantee lower prices? And do so by removing profit out of the equation, do so by actually passing on the savings of rent and property tax directly to customers, and do so through a pilot program of one store in each borough that builds on the feasibility study that was done in Chicago and builds on the successes we’ve seen in actually in Republican states. I think it's time also for Democrats to challenge Republicans on what they say that they believe. Because what we've allowed them to do is set the message and then try and point to statistics as to how it's actually insincere, instead of challenging them.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Alyssa Katz** We have Governor Hochul for now. We have a Democratic legislature for now, that you are in. You also have a legislature and a governor who have really given New York City a raw deal recently. Hochul, I think, is just afraid of New York City — with her reversal on congestion pricing, short-changing the city on environmental bond funds, there's a whole host of ways in which New York City is just not getting a fair deal. So what have you learned by being \[in Albany\] about what it will take to actually build a true coalition at a time when you have a lot of members who are just trying to protect their seats and a governor who is terrified of losing more seats as well? How do you begin that work as a mayor?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** I want to go back to the “Fix the MTA” campaign as an example of this, because I think that it illustrates the exact question you're asking. If you only want to organize with individuals who are like-minded on every single issue, you are not going to win, because we do not have a legislature that reflects any kind of ideological dominance or even a progressive bent.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: What you need to do, in fact, is ask every single person, even those that have previously disagreed with you, to see if there’s any world in which they would support this. I stood side by side with people that I had endorsed primary challenges against in prior years. And I went to those individuals and asked them, “don’t think about me. Think about what this bus could mean in your district.” And I made it clear that what I was asking for in this moment was simply adherence to the vision of this proposal, not anything beyond that in the same way that this campaign is not asking for purity across the board from everyone who supports it, that they are lined up with every single idea and they agree with me on every single thing, but rather that they are aligned on one central message: That New York City should be more affordable.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Alyssa Katz** That’s fine insofar as your own proposals go. But what about the bread-and-butter budget issues and mayoral control \[of city schools\] — there’s a lot that, as you know, a mayor really relies on Albany for authority to do. And the folks in Albany use it as a kind of hostage-taking, as an opportunity to really squeeze that mayor. So how do you break through all of that to to actually build power that, honestly, the mayor doesn’t have?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** I think the first thing you do is that you actually try. There are many years when Eric Adams has been the mayor where his attempts at winning over the legislature have started so late that we are starting to wonder whether they even started at all. And I say this as someone who is looking forward to [“Tin Cup Day”](https://www.thecity.nyc/2024/02/06/eric-adams-albany-tin-cup-day-bqe-school-control-migrants/) next week, where as someone serving on the cities committee, I will have three minutes to question our mayor. But you know, the mayor may not have technical power in Albany, but the mayor has what has been described as the second largest bully pulpit in America. Our mayor has used that bully pulpit to come to Albany on an annual basis to seek, primarily, rollbacks of civil rights protections for Black and brown New Yorkers. That’s what our mayor has focused his energy on. And by and large, he has been successful.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: And yet, as we have seen, no matter the year, no matter the extent of that rollback, New Yorkers do not actually feel a tangible benefit from it. So what I would say to you is that I would use the power of that pulpit to, every single day, make the case as to why our agenda needs to be enacted in Albany.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: When I think about de Blasio and Cuomo, that was a very difficult relationship. And when de Blasio won on a mandate, similarly, of three things — end stop-and-frisk, tax the rich to fund universal pre-K — he needed Albany for two and three. And when he got there, he found a governor that was not interested at all in taxing the rich, and through his partnership and the leadership of the legislature, he was able to put Cuomo in a position where Cuomo found hundreds of millions of dollars in the general fund to make this problem go away.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: And that goes back to the fact that I’m not trying to win as one man crossing the finish line on June 24. I’m trying to win as someone with a mandate specifically about these three issues. And I want people to know that when they vote for me, they’re voting for these issues. So that when elected and I go to Albany, it's clear to everyone that I'm fighting for these things and that the votes that they see across New York City are not necessarily die-hard for Zohran specifically as an individual, but are die hard for freezing the rent, for making buses fast and free, and for bringing universal child care.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: _**NYC Budget / Housing / Rent Freeze / Funding Policy Proposals**_ **Liena Zagare** If elected, you would inherit \[Adams’\] budget. Where do you see the biggest disconnect, and how would you sort of go about trying to modify it, if at all?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** I think the biggest disconnect is that Adams, especially through the OMB \[Office of Management and Budget\], has sought to create an artificial sense of precarity around city finances. And as the mayor, you have an immense power in putting forward the context that people are going to debate how the money should be spent. And Adams artificially reduces that amount of money, therefore forcing people to fight over scraps for many months that then find out at the end there's actually more money than we anticipated. And this isn’t wholly unique to Adams, we see it in Albany as well where the governor's estimates of revenue are quite regularly outpaced by the actual amounts the year later. But Adams does take it to a new level.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: What I would do is to stop playing these kinds of budget games and to make it clear to New Yorkers that they need not expend hours of labor, of time, of anxiety towards fighting for an amount of money they've been told is the maximum they could win, when in fact, there is a far larger pot. And I would also instruct the OMB to continue to be fiscally prudent, but to understand that its mission is also to be honest, to be accurate, and to ensure that agencies can actually staff up.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: Because what I’ve been hearing from countless city workers is how OMB, under Jacques Jiha, has been an impediment to their ability to attract the best and brightest of talent because they will extend job offers and then OMB will then force them to retract that offer, even though it has prior approved that very offer. And if you’re a person looking for a job, and you're forced to wait for two, three, four, five, six months, you are going to go to another job. What we need to do is make it as easy as possible for the best and the brightest across the city to work for the city, and that’s what I’m going to do.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Ben Smith** Is there anything you would spend less money on? **Zohran Mamdani** I would spend less money on the NYPD communications department. **Ben Smith** I saw that in your release, but this is a huge city. That’s a gimmick. That’s five dollars. It is about six or seven people. **Zohran Mamdani** It’s about 80-plus people.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Juan Manuel Benítez** Have you looked at the city budget carefully to fund those huge proposals that you are putting forward, like free buses, no cost for childcare, and also freezing rent-stabilized apartments? You are saying that sometimes you might have to subsidize those landlords that need to make repairs. So where is that money? You say “tax the rich,” that’s going to be pretty hard. It will be really hard, no matter what governor you have there, to pass some sort of tax increase. So where are you finding the money so you can fulfill those really direct promises you’re making in your commercials, like “I’ll make this free for you”?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** I want to go through each one of them. And truly, if you don't feel that I’ve answered it, I want to keep going until I’ve answered it. To be a little more specific about larger-scale dollar numbers, and then I'll go to your question, I also believe that we need to bring down the NYPD’s near billion-dollar overtime. I think that we need to eliminate that overtime. I think that we need to disband the [Strategic Response Group](https://www.nyc.gov/site/nypd/bureaus/patrol/citywide-operations.page). I also believe that we need to cancel the proposal to build what has been dubbed [“Cop City.”](https://citylimits.org/2024/07/09/opinion-mayor-adams-wants-to-turn-nyc-into-cop-city/)

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: Now these are different proposals that I’m putting forward that would save the city hundreds of millions, more than a billion dollars. **Ben Smith** So all the money to fund everything else would come from the NYPD? **Zohran Mamdani** No, that’s not what I'm saying. I’m just providing you with larger scale dollar numbers, in relation to — **Ben Smith** The \[NYPD\] press office — we can all sympathize — but it just seems silly. **Zohran Mamdani** The point of highlighting the press office is more to say that there is somehow always money for certain things and never money for other things. Now New York City’s budget, we’re looking at a budget that is between, what, $114 billion to $117 billion let’s say.

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Harry Siegel** How much of that comes from the feds? **Zohran Mamdani** A substantial number. I’ve seen estimates of a little under 10% coming from the feds. I think there’s a much larger proportion of Albany’s money that is coming from the feds. **Harry Siegel** If that money goes away, right? You’re talking about artificial austerity with Adams and you’re talking about a bigger pie. If there’s significantly less money coming into New York City and New York State from Washington, are there things that need to be cut? What are your priorities for when spending needs to be protected and also still needs to go up? How does that work?

title: Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript
speaker: Mamdani
text: **Zohran Mamdani** Well, what order do you guys want me to answer the questions? **Ben Max** Talk about where you would potentially find savings in the budget. Because it’s all connected. It might be a reduction in federal funding that's forcing you to do that. **Zohran Mamdani** Look, let’s go through this. With the city budget, and I say this as someone who deeply believes in the need for government to provide relief in working-class New Yorkers’ lives. And often, what that looks like is an expansion of what government capacity currently is.
